skillsSnapshot.prompt:
The following skills provide specialized instructions for specific tasks.
Use the read tool to load a skill's file when the task matches its description.
When a skill file references a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory (parent of SKILL.md / dirname of the path) and use that absolute path in tool commands.

<available_skills>
  <skill>
    <name>feishu-doc</name>
    <description>Feishu document read/write operations. Activate when user mentions Feishu docs, cloud docs, or docx links.
</description>
    <location>~/AppData\Roaming\npm\node_modules\openclaw\extensions\feishu\skills\feishu-doc\SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>feishu-drive</name>
    <description>Feishu cloud storage file management. Activate when user mentions cloud space, folders, drive.
</description>
    <location>~/AppData\Roaming\npm\node_modules\openclaw\extensions\feishu\skills\feishu-drive\SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>feishu-perm</name>
    <description>Feishu permission management for documents and files. Activate when user mentions sharing, permissions, collaborators.
</description>
    <location>~/AppData\Roaming\npm\node_modules\openclaw\extensions\feishu\skills\feishu-perm\SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>feishu-wiki</name>
    <description>Feishu knowledge base navigation. Activate when user mentions knowledge base, wiki, or wiki links.
</description>
    <location>~/AppData\Roaming\npm\node_modules\openclaw\extensions\feishu\skills\feishu-wiki\SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>clawhub</name>
    <description>Use the ClawHub CLI to search, install, update, and publish agent skills from clawhub.com. Use when you need to fetch new skills on the fly, sync installed skills to latest or a specific version, or publish new/updated skill folders with the npm-installed clawhub CLI.</description>
    <location>~/AppData\Roaming\npm\node_modules\openclaw\skills\clawhub\SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>coding-agent</name>
    <description>Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Claude Code: use --print --permission-mode bypassPermissions (no PTY). Codex/Pi/OpenCode: pty:true required.</description>
    <location>~/AppData\Roaming\npm\node_modules\openclaw\skills\coding-agent\SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>healthcheck</name>
    <description>Host security hardening and risk-tolerance configuration for OpenClaw deployments. Use when a user asks for security audits, firewall/SSH/update hardening, risk posture, exposure review, OpenClaw cron scheduling for periodic checks, or version status checks on a machine running OpenClaw (laptop, workstation, Pi, VPS).</description>
    <location>~/AppData\Roaming\npm\node_modules\openclaw\skills\healthcheck\SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>node-connect</name>
    <description>Diagnose OpenClaw node connection and pairing failures for Android, iOS, and macOS companion apps. Use when QR/setup code/manual connect fails, local Wi-Fi works but VPS/tailnet does not, or errors mention pairing required, unauthorized, bootstrap token invalid or expired, gateway.bind, gateway.remote.url, Tailscale, or plugins.entries.device-pair.config.publicUrl.</description>
    <location>~/AppData\Roaming\npm\node_modules\openclaw\skills\node-connect\SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>skill-creator</name>
    <description>Create, edit, improve, or audit AgentSkills. Use when creating a new skill from scratch or when asked to improve, review, audit, tidy up, or clean up an existing skill or SKILL.md file. Also use when editing or restructuring a skill directory (moving files to references/ or scripts/, removing stale content, validating against the AgentSkills spec). Triggers on phrases like "create a skill", "author a skill", "tidy up a skill", "improve this skill", "review the skill", "clean up the skill", "audit the skill".</description>
    <location>~/AppData\Roaming\npm\node_modules\openclaw\skills\skill-creator\SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>weather</name>
    <description>Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.</description>
    <location>~/AppData\Roaming\npm\node_modules\openclaw\skills\weather\SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>Agent Browser</name>
    <description>A fast Rust-based headless browser automation CLI with Node.js fallback that enables AI agents to navigate, click, type, and snapshot pages via structured commands.</description>
    <location>~/.openclaw\workspace\skills\Agent browser\SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>ai-boss-assistant</name>
    <description>Transform any AI into a professional executive assistant with battle-tested personas and workflows. Complete templates for Google Workspace integration (Gmail, Calendar, Drive), milestone delivery system, and security guidelines.</description>
    <location>~/.openclaw\workspace\skills\ai-boss-assistant\SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>ai-intelligent-ad-optimization</name>
    <description>广告优化，投放优化 + 创意优化。</description>
    <location>~/.openclaw\workspace\skills\ai-intelligent-ad-optimization\SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>ai-intelligent-chatbot-builder</name>
    <description>聊天机器人，可视化配置 + 多渠道部署。</description>
    <location>~/.openclaw\workspace\skills\ai-intelligent-chatbot-builder\SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>AI-NEWS</name>
    <description>当需要梳理某段时间内AI行业重要新闻时，可以调用使用这个技能。</description>
    <location>~/.openclaw\workspace\skills\ai-news-research\SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>auto-ai-web</name>
    <description>Skill per generare codice e immagini tramite interfaccia web di Claude AI e Gemini quando le API non sono disponibili. Si attiva automaticamente quando l'utente richiede generazione di immagini o scrittura/analisi di codice.</description>
    <location>~/.openclaw\workspace\skills\auto-ai-web\SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>daily-hot-push</name>
    <description>每日推送中国热榜TOP10到飞书。从微博、知乎、百度、36氪筛选最重要的新闻，智能排除明星八卦和主观评论。</description>
    <location>~/.openclaw\workspace\skills\daily-hot-push\SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>Data Analysis</name>
    <description>Data analysis and visualization. Query databases, generate reports, automate spreadsheets, and turn raw data into clear, actionable insights. Use when (1) you need to analyze, visualize, or explain data; (2) the user wants reports, dashboards, or metrics turned into a decision; (3) the work involves SQL, Python, spreadsheets, BI tools, or notebooks; (4) you need to compare segments, cohorts, funnels, experiments, or time periods; (5) the user explicitly installs or references the skill for the current task.</description>
    <location>~/.openclaw\workspace\skills\data-analysis-1.0.2\SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>personal-productivity</name>
    <description>个人效率与时间管理顾问。帮助用户更好地管理时间、任务和能量，提升工作效率。当用户询问如何提高效率、时间管理、任务规划、专注力、拖延症等问题时触发。</description>
    <location>~/.openclaw\workspace\skills\personal-productivity\SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>productivity-bot</name>
    <description>Automation bot for productivity tasks including data processing, scheduled notifications, and workflow optimization.</description>
    <location>~/.openclaw\workspace\skills\productivity-bot\SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>self-improvement</name>
    <description>Captures learnings, errors, and corrections to enable continuous improvement. Use when: (1) A command or operation fails unexpectedly, (2) User corrects Claude ('No, that's wrong...', 'Actually...'), (3) User requests a capability that doesn't exist, (4) An external API or tool fails, (5) Claude realizes its knowledge is outdated or incorrect, (6) A better approach is discovered for a recurring task. Also review learnings before major tasks.</description>
    <location>~/.openclaw\workspace\skills\Self improvement\SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>skill-vetter</name>
    <description>Security-first skill vetting for AI agents. Use before installing any skill from ClawdHub, GitHub, or other sources. Checks for red flags, permission scope, and suspicious patterns.</description>
    <location>~/.openclaw\workspace\skills\skill-vetter-1.0.0\SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>find-skills</name>
    <description>Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like "how do I do X", "find a skill for X", "is there a skill that can...", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill.</description>
    <location>~/.openclaw\workspace\skills\SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>twitter-post</name>
    <description>Post tweets to Twitter/X via the official API v2 (OAuth 1.0a). Use when the user asks to tweet, post to Twitter/X, send a thread, reply to a tweet, or quote tweet. Supports single tweets, threads, replies, and quote tweets with automatic character weight validation.</description>
    <location>~/.openclaw\workspace\skills\twitter-post\SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>web-automation-helper</name>
    <description>浏览器自动化助手。通过Chrome远程调试模式，自动化执行网页操作，包括数据抓取、表单填写、内容发布、截图等。</description>
    <location>~/.openclaw\workspace\skills\web-automation-helper\SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>web-pilot</name>
    <description>Search the web and read page contents without API keys. Use when you need to search via DuckDuckGo/Brave/Google (multi-page), extract readable text from URLs, browse interactively with a persistent visible browser (with tabs, click, screenshot, text search), download files/PDFs, or dismiss cookie banners. Supports JSON/markdown/text output. Powered by Playwright + Chromium.</description>
    <location>~/.openclaw\workspace\skills\web-pilot\SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>wechat-article-publisher</name>
    <description>从 Markdown 文件或网页链接提取文章并发布到微信公众号。适用于需要“自动创建草稿/提交发布”、统一微信样式（standard/viral）和批量复用发布流程的场景。</description>
    <location>~/.openclaw\workspace\skills\wechat-article-publisher\SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>wechat-mp-reader</name>
    <description>Read WeChat official account articles. Use the built-in browser tool to open the page and extract body text. Always append ?scene=1 to the URL.</description>
    <location>~/.openclaw\workspace\skills\wechat-mp-reader\SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
</available_skills>

resolvedSkills.name: coding-agent
resolvedSkills.description: Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Claude Code: use --print --permission-mode bypassPermissions (no PTY). Codex/Pi/OpenCode: pty:true required.

resolvedSkills.name: coding-agent
resolvedSkills.description: Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Claude Code: use --print --permission-mode bypassPermissions (no PTY). Codex/Pi/OpenCode: pty:true required.

resolvedSkills.name: clawhub
resolvedSkills.description: Use the ClawHub CLI to search, install, update, and publish agent skills from clawhub.com. Use when you need to fetch new skills on the fly, sync installed skills to latest or a specific version, or publish new/updated skill folders with the npm-installed clawhub CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: healthcheck
resolvedSkills.description: Host security hardening and risk-tolerance configuration for OpenClaw deployments. Use when a user asks for security audits, firewall/SSH/update hardening, risk posture, exposure review, OpenClaw cron scheduling for periodic checks, or version status checks on a machine running OpenClaw (laptop, workstation, Pi, VPS).

resolvedSkills.name: node-connect
resolvedSkills.description: Diagnose OpenClaw node connection and pairing failures for Android, iOS, and macOS companion apps. Use when QR/setup code/manual connect fails, local Wi-Fi works but VPS/tailnet does not, or errors mention pairing required, unauthorized, bootstrap token invalid or expired, gateway.bind, gateway.remote.url, Tailscale, or plugins.entries.device-pair.config.publicUrl.

resolvedSkills.name: skill-creator
resolvedSkills.description: Create, edit, improve, or audit AgentSkills. Use when creating a new skill from scratch or when asked to improve, review, audit, tidy up, or clean up an existing skill or SKILL.md file. Also use when editing or restructuring a skill directory (moving files to references/ or scripts/, removing stale content, validating against the AgentSkills spec). Triggers on phrases like "create a skill", "author a skill", "tidy up a skill", "improve this skill", "review the skill", "clean up the skill", "audit the skill".

resolvedSkills.name: weather
resolvedSkills.description: Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.

resolvedSkills.name: Agent Browser
resolvedSkills.description: A fast Rust-based headless browser automation CLI with Node.js fallback that enables AI agents to navigate, click, type, and snapshot pages via structured commands.

resolvedSkills.name: Data Analysis
resolvedSkills.description: Data analysis and visualization. Query databases, generate reports, automate spreadsheets, and turn raw data into clear, actionable insights. Use when (1) you need to analyze, visualize, or explain data; (2) the user wants reports, dashboards, or metrics turned into a decision; (3) the work involves SQL, Python, spreadsheets, BI tools, or notebooks; (4) you need to compare segments, cohorts, funnels, experiments, or time periods; (5) the user explicitly installs or references the skill for the current task.

resolvedSkills.name: self-improvement
resolvedSkills.description: Captures learnings, errors, and corrections to enable continuous improvement. Use when: (1) A command or operation fails unexpectedly, (2) User corrects Claude ('No, that's wrong...', 'Actually...'), (3) User requests a capability that doesn't exist, (4) An external API or tool fails, (5) Claude realizes its knowledge is outdated or incorrect, (6) A better approach is discovered for a recurring task. Also review learnings before major tasks.

resolvedSkills.name: find-skills
resolvedSkills.description: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like "how do I do X", "find a skill for X", "is there a skill that can...", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill.

resolvedSkills.description: Captures learnings, errors, and corrections to enable continuous improvement. Use when: (1) A command or operation fails unexpectedly, (2) User corrects Claude ('No, that's wrong...', 'Actually...'), (3) User requests a capability that doesn't exist, (4) An external API or tool fails, (5) Claude realizes its knowledge is outdated or incorrect, (6) A better approach is discovered for a recurring task. Also review learnings before major tasks.

resolvedSkills.name: skill-vetter
resolvedSkills.description: Security-first skill vetting for AI agents. Use before installing any skill from ClawdHub, GitHub, or other sources. Checks for red flags, permission scope, and suspicious patterns.

resolvedSkills.name: find-skills
resolvedSkills.description: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like "how do I do X", "find a skill for X", "is there a skill that can...", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill.

resolvedSkills.name: twitter-post
resolvedSkills.description: Post tweets to Twitter/X via the official API v2 (OAuth 1.0a). Use when the user asks to tweet, post to Twitter/X, send a thread, reply to a tweet, or quote tweet. Supports single tweets, threads, replies, and quote tweets with automatic character weight validation.

resolvedSkills.name: web-automation-helper
resolvedSkills.description: 浏览器自动化助手。通过Chrome远程调试模式，自动化执行网页操作，包括数据抓取、表单填写、内容发布、截图等。

resolvedSkills.name: web-pilot
resolvedSkills.description: Search the web and read page contents without API keys. Use when you need to search via DuckDuckGo/Brave/Google (multi-page), extract readable text from URLs, browse interactively with a persistent visible browser (with tabs, click, screenshot, text search), download files/PDFs, or dismiss cookie banners. Supports JSON/markdown/text output. Powered by Playwright + Chromium.

resolvedSkills.name: wechat-article-publisher
resolvedSkills.description: 从 Markdown 文件或网页链接提取文章并发布到微信公众号。适用于需要“自动创建草稿/提交发布”、统一微信样式（standard/viral）和批量复用发布流程的场景。

resolvedSkills.name: wechat-mp-reader
resolvedSkills.description: Read WeChat official account articles. Use the built-in browser tool to open the page and extract body text. Always append ?scene=1 to the URL.
